title: SREM
description: Removes one or more members from a set. Deletes the set if the last member was removed.
complexity: O(N) where N is the number of members to be removed.

returns.description: 1 if the new element was removed, 0 if the new element was not a member of the set

jedis.signature: long srem(final byte[] key, final byte[]... members)

returns.description: 1 if the new element was removed, 0 if the new element was not a member of the set

jedis.signature: long srem(final String key, final String... members)

returns.description: Long integer-reply the number of members that were removed from the set, not including non existing members.
Long integer-reply the number of members that were removed from the set, not including non existing members.
Long integer-reply the number of members that were removed from the set, not including non existing members.

srem(...args: [, key: RedisKey, members: (string | Buffer | number)[], callback: Callback<number>, ])

params.name: *members

returns.description: The number of members that were successfully removed

content:
Remove the specified members from the set stored at `key`. Specified members that are not a member of this set are ignored. If `key` does not exist, it is treated as an empty set and this command returns `0`. An error is returned when the value stored at `key` is not a set. ## Examples {{% redis-cli %}} SADD myset "one" SADD myset "two" SADD myset "three" SREM myset "one" SREM myset "four" SMEMBERS myset {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="srem-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of members that were removed from the set, not including non existing members. -tab-sep- [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): Number of members that were removed from the set, not including non existing members. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
SADD myset "one"
SADD myset "two"
SADD myset "three"
SREM myset "one"
SREM myset "four"
SMEMBERS myset
intro:
Remove the specified members from the set stored at `key`.
Specified members that are not a member of this set are ignored.
If `key` does not exist, it is treated as an empty set and this command returns
`0`.

An error is returned when the value stored at `key` is not a set.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Remove the specified members from the set stored at `key`.
Specified members that are not a member of this set are ignored.
If `key` does not exist, it is treated as an empty set and this command returns
`0`.

An error is returned when the value stored at `key` is not a set.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
{{% redis-cli %}}
SADD myset "one"
SADD myset "two"
SADD myset "three"
SREM myset "one"
SREM myset "four"
SMEMBERS myset
{{% /redis-cli %}}

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |
